tree.name: 0) Store grid-scale renewable energy
tree.description: Develop novel methods to store grid-scale renewable energy that do not rely on rare-earth lithium-ion batteries, targeting cost, scalability, and environmental sustainability

achievers.name: Material Re-users & Recyclers
achievers.description: This stakeholder group comprises companies and organizations specializing in the collection, processing, and re-purposing of materials from retired energy storage systems. Their expertise is crucial for designing systems with end-of-life considerations, ensuring environmental sustainability, and potentially lowering overall material costs through circular economy practices.

achievers.name: Maximize Efficient System Material Reclamation
achievers.description: This goal represents a state where the design of novel energy storage systems inherently supports the highest possible rate and ease of material recovery at their end-of-life. It ensures that components and materials can be efficiently dismantled, separated, and processed for re-entry into manufacturing streams, reducing waste and reliance on virgin resources.

achievers.name: Digital Material Passport & Disassembly Twin
achievers.description:
Implement a 'Digital Material Passport & Disassembly Twin' for every grid-scale energy storage system. This solution embeds a comprehensive digital record containing detailed material composition, assembly instructions, and optimal disassembly pathways directly onto each unit, enabling Material Re-users & Recyclers to access precise, system-specific guidance for reclamation.

This dramatically increases the efficiency and accuracy of material reclamation by eliminating guesswork and manual sorting. Recyclers can instantly identify valuable materials, hazardous components, and the most efficient methods for their separation, reducing labor costs and improving material purity for re-manufacturing.

achievers.name: Modular Design Universal Tooling Interface
achievers.description: {'What is the solution?': "Implement a 'Modular Design with Universal Tooling Interface' for all novel energy storage systems. This involves designing systems as easily separable, standardized modules using a limited set of universal, non-destructive fasteners and connection points that are explicitly accessible by standard automated or semi-automated tools.", 'Why is it a good solution?': 'This inherent design principle drastically reduces the time and specialized equipment needed for system dismantling, enabling rapid mechanical separation of components. It allows Material Re-users & Recyclers to develop highly efficient, standardized disassembly lines and automated processes, significantly lowering labor costs and increasing material throughput and purity for re-manufacturing.'}

achievers.name: Reversible Bonding & Disaggregation
achievers.description:
Implement 'Reversible Bonding and Disaggregation Materials' in novel energy storage system designs. This involves utilizing advanced, controlled-release adhesives, phase-change materials, or chemically reversible bonds that, upon reaching end-of-life conditions (e.g., specific pH, heat, solvent application), weaken or dissolve, allowing components to naturally separate.

This approach bypasses complex mechanical disassembly challenges, enabling highly efficient, non-destructive separation of intricate assemblies into their constituent material streams. Material Re-users & Recyclers can apply targeted chemical or thermal processes for clean component disaggregation, leading to purer material fractions and significantly reduced manual labor and energy costs compared to traditional methods.

achievers.name: Embedded Material ID for Automated Sorting
achievers.description:
Design novel energy storage components with embedded, machine-readable material markers (e.g., RFID tags, unique spectral signatures, or microscopic barcodes) directly integrated during manufacturing. These markers encode precise material composition, grade, and optimal reclamation pathways.
This enables highly efficient, high-throughput automated sorting systems at end-of-life, allowing for rapid and accurate identification and separation of diverse material streams. It drastically reduces manual labor, eliminates sorting errors, and ensures the recovery of high-purity material fractions for re-entry into manufacturing.
